observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventory items by type and reported in the RPCI to be reconciled with accounting records and submitted to the audit team.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what have transpired during the process and make representation with the DSWD as to the proper action on this matter. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit explanation/justification on non-compliance with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.

Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation for the non-submission/lack of documentary requirements on the Small Value Procurements.  Likewise, submit the supporting documents such as PhilGEPS Registration, Statement of the Prospective bidder that it is not blacklisted, and Sworn affidavit, Mayor’s/Business Permit, PhilGEPS Registration Number, Income/Business Tax Return, and Omnibus Sworn Statement required in Appendix A and COA Circular 2012-001 to establish validity of the claims.

We recommended that the management submit a written explanation for the non-submission/lack of documentary requirements on the Negotiated Procurements (Emergency cases).  Likewise, submit the supporting documents such as Mayor’s/Business Permit, Income/Business Tax Return, and Omnibus Sworn Statement required in Appendix A of Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to establish validity of the claims.
We recommended and Management agreed to provide the audit team with updated/supplemental LDRRMFIP and/or AIP to support the disbursements for Covid-19 response taken from the LDRRMF.
We recommended that management direct the Property Officer to prepare the PAR instead of ARE in the issuances of property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 259 of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant require the aforementioned supporting documents from all departments that facilitated the receipt and distribution of cash and in-kind donations, prepare the required summary/list and submit to the audit team with the supporting documents. observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant affix his signature/certification on the computations of the terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to signify review made by the Accounting Unit and that claims for terminal leave benefits be supported with the complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: In their written reply received on March 24, 2017, they reasoned that the terminal pay computations of the HRMO for eight officials and employees were not certified by the Accountant because they were unaware of the said COA Circular No. 2012-001. Further, they were also unaware of the authorization in affidavit form to deduct all financial obligations with the LGU and affidavit of the applicants that there is no pending criminal investigation or prosecution against them. In view of the above, they are asking for reconsideration and will strictly observe the said requirements in the future.
observations.reason: Recommendation was complied for the succeeding transactions.

observations.recommendation: Submit the specified documents or a written explanation why the same could not be submitted.
observations.managementAction: List of beneficiaries with signatures and a copy of OR upon receipt of funds were some of the documents already submitted on March 24, 2017. MOA/Trust Agreement with Cebu Province, BAC Resolution recommending the use of Agency-to-Agency Agreement to the HOPE and MOA with the Servicing Agency were not submitted on the presumption that Agency to Agency procurement need not require the BAC resolution. They were asking for consideration and vowed to strictly observe the regulations in future transactions. A request for re-alignment of the unutilized balance of fund transfer was submitted to the provincial government but there was no reply yet.
observations.reason: Documents were submitted together with the explanation on deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: Facilitate completion of the 10 year solid waste management plan. Likewise, management should comply with all of the applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing the open dump site, develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and residual wastes of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Per Resolution No. 850 series of 2016 dated January 12, 2017 The dumpsite was already closed effective January 2017 and the construction of the Materials Recovery Facility is on-going.
observations.reason: The Per Resolution recommendations were already complied.

observations.recommendation: BAC Secretariat submit evidences of invitation of observers in all stages of the procurement process or a written explanation on why such procedural requirement was not complied.
observations.managementAction: Municipal officials claimed that the last time they sent invitation to COA to observe the bidding process was on February 4, 2016. Since they had not received a reply to any of those requests, they presumed that due to the said voluminous tasks assigned, the audit team had no more time to read the requests much more attend the proceedings. They are glad that the matter was brought up and are sending again those invitations to COA. However, they always invited representatives from private sector to witness those proceedings and added that they share the Commission’s vision of transparency, accountability and participatory process in conducting public biddings.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat are now back to sending invitation to COA in addition to other observers.

observations.recommendation: Assess the cost of gender mainstreaming in a flagship/regular program using the Harmonized GAD Guidelines pursuant to Item 6.4 of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied. Attached in their 2017 Annual Budget is the certification from the DILG that their GAD Plan and Budget was found compliant in form and contents with the provision of the JMC 2016-01.
observations.reason: Attached in their 2017 Annual Budget is the certification from the DILG that their GAD Plan and Budget was found compliant in form and contents with the provision of the JMC 2016-01.

observations.recommendation: Direct GFPS to formulate the Local GAD Code with the assistance of DILG and PCW.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied.
observations.reason: GAD Code was formulated, per SB Resolution No. 2016-122 dated November 2, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 on the prompt submission of contracts and supporting documents, purchase orders, work orders and notices of deliveries to ensure timely evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat agreed to submit the required documents directly to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts for CY 2017 were already received within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Intensify implementation of the solid waste management program employing the use of the 3 sets Composter Drum with Shredder to ensure that benefit is felt by the constituents before the subject equipment will be destroyed by elements in the dumpsite and put government funds to waste. Furnish the audit team with a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission, the Order creating the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and a copy of the plan and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Composter Drums were already utilized for the biodegradable wastes. The agency is paying a private garbage dumpsite in the City of Naga for their plastic wastes.
observations.reason: The recommendation was complied.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Chief Executive ensure optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment and fast-track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as required under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 as amended by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Their municipality was shortlisted in 2016 and an SGLG awardee in 2017.
observations.reason: The municipality has complied with the minimum percentage in the implementation of development projects which is 60% as stipulated by the DILG to qualify for the Seal of Good Local Governance.

observations.recommendation: Cause the submission of LDRRMFIP prepared in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Plan was prepared in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: The recommendation was already complied.

observations.recommendation: Submit a written explanation on what has transpired which afforded the contractors to complete the projects stated in record time of two-three days.
observations.managementAction: Some contractors will mobilize their equipment and have some of the materials delivered if they knew they submitted the lowest bid and start the project at the time the contract is signed. They had also done the project in a faster pace since there was a ban of payments of government projects due to the general elections on May 2016 and will start 3rd week of March 2016. However, upon inspection, they said that the integrity and strength of the concrete pavements were not compromised even if they were done in a shorter time. They just made sure that the curing time in which the road can be used was observed and the specifications strictly followed.
observations.reason: Some contractors will mobilize their equipment and have some of the materials January 2017. This observation was still noted in transactions.

observations.recommendation: Fast-track the implementation of the planned DRRM programs and ensure that the LDRRMFIP is itemized in the prescribed COA Circular format with specific projects and activities listed therein and incorporated in the LGU’s local development plan and annual work and financial plan.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Office of Samboan acknowledged the utilization of 5.9% only of its DRRM funds and manifested that Samboan will spend its funds to improve resiliency/capacity against hazards and disaster it might face in the future by purchasing more vehicles, equipment tools and educational campaigns.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Officer acknowledged minimal utilization and plans to spend funds for improvements.

observations.recommendation: Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to correct the entries made for the said loan received from the President’s Social Fund. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the amount enough to pay the amortization of P937,500.00 and the accrued interest of P186,562.50 and the Municipal Accountant should prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the interest expense and payable in the books.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant had not yet set-up the payable account; the municipal accountant should prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the entries.
observations.reason: Accrual of interest expense is not yet booked up.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountant to submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the general ledger balances.  Henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Only six general ledger accounts were supported with schedules.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Office and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer maintain close coordination and reconcile their records regularly so that differences could be immediately corrected.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was conducted for CY 2015 but the RPCPPE was only submitted on May 5, 2016.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the General Ledger balances and Property Records is not conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Submit an explanation on why full payment was made despite deficiency in workmanship and require the contractor to correct the defects discovered in the project pursuant to Section 62 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;

Direct those concerned employees to exercise due care and diligence in the inspection, evaluation and acceptance of projects turned over by the contractor; and

Instruct concerned officials to submit the following documents:

Results of Eligibility Check/Screening
Minutes of Pre-Bid Conference
Post-Qualification Report of Technical Working Group
Notice of Post-Qualification
Post-Qualification Evaluation Report
Approved Budget for the Contract
Evidence of Invitation of one more observer in all stages of the procurement process (besides COA and SAFSA President)
Inspection Report by the Agency’s Authorized Engineer
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were not complied.
observations.reason: No explanation/justification as well as required supporting documents were received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive ensure optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment and fast-track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as required under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 as amended by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the concerned officials/employees immediately cause the submission of all the required financial accounts and reports– vouchers, bank reconciliation statements, official receipts promptly and strictly adhere to the cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submission of accounts and reports is still way beyond the deadlines set.
observations.reason: CY 2016 accounts and reports are still submitted beyond the prescribed periods.  Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management utilize LDRRMF to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment were procured so as to build the disaster resilience of communities and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: Of the 10 DRRM programs, only two were implemented for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Utilization of LDRRMF in CY 2016 is only 15%.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant or other responsible official of the municipality to prepare separate Registry for Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for LDRRMF to improve the availability, reliability and quality of financial information on disaster funds.
observations.managementAction: No RAAOs for LDRRMF were prepared.
observations.reason: RAAOs were still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assess the cost of gender mainstreaming in a flagship/regular program using the Harmonized GAD Guidelines pursuant to Item 6.4 of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was not complied.
observations.reason: Gender analysis of programs and projects supporting gender mainstreaming through the administration of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) is still not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management through its GFPS formulate the Local GAD Code with the assistance of DILG and PCW.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was not complied.
observations.reason: GAD Code was still not formulated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials and employees refund the amount received in excess of the allowed PEI of P5,000.00 for CY 2014.
observations.managementAction: Refund was not yet made.  The disallowance was already booked up.
observations.reason: Notice of Disallowance No. 2015-002-101 (2014) dated April 17, 2015 was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unspent PDAF funds be returned to the National Treasury so that it can be used for the other priority projects of the government.
observations.managementAction: The management is planning to use the unspent PDAF for their scholarship program.  They added that the LGU will only return if the Cebu Provincial Government will return their own unspent PDAF.
observations.reason: Unspent PDAF are still not returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that copies of the contract documents shall be furnished to the Auditor not later than five days from perfection and duly accomplished supporting documents shall be required before payment to establish the validity of the claims.  Nonetheless, contract documents for transactions awarded through competitive bidding in 2014 should be submitted to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Government contracts and duly accomplished supporting documents were submitted beyond the prescribed period, but were inspected by the COA Technical personnel.
observations.reason: Government contracts were still not submitted within the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the municipal accountant shall coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office for the copies of documents representing the donated materials and prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to take up the following necessary adjusting entries in the municipal books of accounts in 2009, to wit:
observations.managementAction: Construction materials were donated to the LGU without proper notification and turn-over of pertinent documents. Thus, the Municipal Accountant is hesitant in implementing the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still did not coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office and   adjusting entries were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: The concerned officials and employees should refund the extra cash gift in the amount of P505,600.00 and P610,000.00
observations.managementAction:
They reiterated the provision of LBC No. 75 particularly on Item No.4.

Justification by management was allegedly submitted to previous auditors but a copy is not available for evaluation.
observations.reason: No refund was made.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make representation to the Sanguniang Bayan to pass a resolution requesting for write-off of dormant and uncollectible accounts receivables addressed to the Adjudication and Settlement Board of the Commission on Audit supported with the following documents in accordance with Item III. A.10 of COA Circular No. made on the accounts; and
Certification and reasons 07-001:
List of available records and extent of validation  why the records, schedules and supporting vouchers/documents cannot be located
observations.managementAction: No request for write-off was submitted to the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: The request for write off by the municipality is not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation:
The concerned officials should be required to refund the excess payment of their RATA and management should refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time the Personal Services limitation had been observed pursuant to the provisions cited in LBC No. 84 dated April 13, 2007.

Again, it is advised that management should take into consideration the review comments of the Provincial Finance Committee on their budgets before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.
observations.managementAction:
They reiterated the provision of LBC No. 75 particularly on Item No. 4

Agency claimed that the PBO did not submit accurate computation.
observations.reason: No refund was made.

observations.recommendation: Inform immediately the Office of the President of the non-implementation of the President’s Social Fund of P1.0 M and the decision to return the same as well as request for condonation of interest charges. Also, request for realignment or use of the funds for a project/program that would really be feasible in the municipality and beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor is still looking for feasible project before requesting for realignment to the Office of the President.
observations.reason: The municipality has not informed the Office of the President of the non-implementation of the project and their plan not to return the amount and request for condonation of interest is not submitted to the Office of the President.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that guideline on the grant of honoraria to the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat as provided for under Budget Circular 2004-5 dated March 23, 2004, especially on the funding source, should be strictly followed. Otherwise, concerned officials should refund the honoraria paid and charges against fund outside of the authorized funding source.
observations.managementAction: The agency has already followed the guidelines set for the payment of honoraria to BAC members. To date no refund was made because according to them a request for reconsideration to ND#2004-001-100 (2004) L2–06-153-36-005 dated February 17, 2006 has been sent to the Commission on Audit through the Regional Office.
observations.reason: Management is waiting for the result of their request for reconsideration before they make any refund.

observations.recommendation: Require the MPDC/Municipal Engineer to submit to the municipal accountant all documents received by him relative to donations for the latter to book it up in the books of accounts. Likewise, issuance of said materials to be used for the projects should also be reported by the MPDC/Municipal Engineer for proper recording. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to book up the materials received from the World Vision International by debiting the account Waterways, Aqueducts, Seawalls, River Walls and Other (257) and crediting the account Prior Year’s Adjustment (684).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not draw a JEV to book up the materials received from the MPDC. The MPDC reasoned out that said materials were installed to the barangay waterworks system of Barangay Monteverde and the project was already transferred to them for management and maintenance, so the municipality had no more control of the project.
observations.reason: JEV was not drawn to book up the donated materials.

observations.recommendation: Require the MPDC/Municipal Engineer to submit to the municipal accountant inventory of donated materials received and the documents relative to donations for the latter to book it up in the books of accounts. Likewise, issuance of said materials to be used for the projects should also be reported by the MPDC/Municipal Engineer for proper recording. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to books up the materials received from the World Vision International by debiting the Construction Materials Inventory and crediting the account Prior Year’s Adjustment.
observations.managementAction:
The MPDC reasoned out that it will be Barangay Jumangpas which will book up the materials since the municipality only acted as conduit and provided technical assistance in its implementation. Proper recording will be done at the barangay level since it is the barangay who now owns the project.

The municipality still has to book up the donated materials since it was the original donee and then properly document the transfer of the materials to barangay Jumangpas.
observations.reason: JEV was not drawn to book up the donated materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE and will prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for submission to the concerned auditor.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to prepare and submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the general ledger balances and to maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of accounts in the financial statements.
We recommend that management submit evidences of invitation of observers in all stages of the procurement process or a written explanation on why such procedural requirement was not complied.
We, therefore, recommend that management submit a written explanation on what has transpired which afforded the contractors to complete the projects stated in record time of two-three days.

We, therefore, recommend that the Municipal Accountant affix his signature/certification on the computations of the HRMO of the terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to signify review made by the Accounting Unit and that claims for terminal leave benefits be supported with the complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommend that management submit the above-specified documents or a written explanation why the same could not be submitted.
We recommend that the management submit the above-specified documentary requirements or a written explanation why such could not be submitted.
We, therefore, recommend that management submit approved contracts and supporting documents within five (5) days after its execution and properly substantiate subsequent payments.

We recommend that management appropriately substantiate the aforementioned acquisition of land and give preferential attention to the titling of the said parcels of land as well as the other untitled lots owned by the LGU.  Also, funds, manpower and logistics should be allocated for the immediate titling of these lots in the name of the Municipality of Samboan.

We recommend that management comply  with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 96-010 dated August 15, 1996  on the prompt submission of contracts and supporting documents, purchase orders, work orders and notices of deliveries to ensure timely evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommend that management fast-track the implementation of the planned DRRM programs to be itemized in the LDRRMFIP prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 with specific projects and activities listed therein incorporated in the LGU’s local development plan and annual work and financial plan  to achieve the desired disaster resiliency of the community and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommend that management facilitate the completion of the 10 year solid waste management plan.  Likewise, management should comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing the open dump site, develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and eventually, residual wastes of the municipality.

We recommend that the requisitioning officers for the procurement of drugs and medicines be required to certify as to its conformity with the essential drugs list as contained in the Philippines National Drug Formulary, to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public.
observations.managementAction: Certification as to conformity with PNDF was submitted.  Likewise, prospective procurement for drugs and medicines are already supported with said certification.
observations.reason: LGU’s procurement of drugs and medicines in 2016 were already supported with the required certification as to conformity with PNDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountant to record medical supplies, drugs and medicines purchased for operations as inventory accounts and to credit the same for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal using the appropriate accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Medical supplies, drugs and medicines procured are already recorded as inventories.
observations.reason: Procurement of drugs and medicines in CY 2016 were recorded as inventories

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management withhold taxes on the payment of honoraria to personnel in accordance with Section 2.79 of RR No. 2-98 as amended by Section 3 of RR No. 10-2008.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, taxes were already withheld from payment of honoraria to the staff of the municipality’s health facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification/explanation with supporting documents for the variances specified for evaluation by the COA Regional Technical Services, otherwise the differences shall be personal liabilities of the persons determined liable.
observations.managementAction: Justification/explanation were submitted last May 18, 2016.
observations.reason: Justification/ explanation will still be verified by COA technical.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant include discussion/ disclosure of the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Notes to Financial Statements included information on LDRRMF, however, unexpended balances transferred to Special Trust Fund were not presented by year.
observations.reason: Unexpended balances transferred to Special Trust Fund were not presented by year as required in the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management cause the submission of LDRRMFIP prepared in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Plan was prepared but not in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: The prescribed format of LDRRMFIP was not used.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that LGU:

Intensify implementation of the solid waste management program employing the use of the 3 sets Composter Drum with Shredder to ensure that benefit is felt by the constituents before the subject equipment will be destroyed by elements in the dumpsite and put government funds to waste.

Furnish the audit team with a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and a copy of the order creating the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board.
observations.managementAction:
Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted for approval to the National Solid Waste Commission. The SWM Board was already created.

Electrical connections are already being prepared for utilization of the Composter Drums in the near future.
observations.reason: Composter drum is still not utilized for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should intensify the collection of overdue loan accounts and/or implement the stipulation per loan agreement which is to collect the produce of the farmer loaners or their “hayupan” to pay the unpaid loan balance.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant informed that despite the demand letters, most of the borrowers still refuse to pay their loans. He also informed that the loan balance has already decreased to P31,000.00.
observations.reason: The receivable was not fully collected.

observations.recommendation: In the meantime that the municipality has yet to receive a directive from the Office of the President for the return of the P1,000,0000.00 soft loan, the municipal accountant should prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the entries made for the said loan received from the President’s Social Fund. Furthermore, require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the amount enough to pay the amortization of P937,500.00 and the accrued interest of P186,562.50 and the Municipal Accountant should prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the interest expense and payable in the books.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant had already set-up the domestic loans payable account.
